纽约（CNN）脸谱网（Facebook）指控与法国军方有联系的人周二针对非洲部分地区开展了秘密的在线势力行动。这是Facebook首次将这样的活动公开链接到与西方军方有联系的个人。
 据称使用的欺骗手段，包括使用Facebook在目标国家/地区摆弄当地人的形象，反映了俄罗斯政府开展的虚假宣传活动。
 Facebook工作人员在周二的一次新闻发布会上告诉记者，该公司无法说出该行动是否由法国军方自己指挥-他们只是说这是由“个人关联公司”经营的。与军队。
   根据Facebook的说法，这些行动的目标是中非共和国和马里，以及程度较小的尼日尔，布基纳法索，阿尔及利亚，科特迪瓦和乍得。
 Facebook删除了这些帐户，并在周二还宣布已删除与俄罗斯巨魔集团有联系的，也冒充非洲人的帐户。
  Facebook说，在某些情况下，伪造的法国和俄罗斯账户甚至相互影响。
 CNN先前报道说，活跃在非洲的俄罗斯特工与同一个俄罗斯巨魔集团有联系，后者据称在2016年美国总统大选前夕利用社交媒体冒充美国人。 
Facebook说，所谓的法国账户主要是用法语和阿拉伯语发布的，涉及新闻和时事，包括法国在非洲法语国家的政策，非洲各个国家的安全局势，声称俄罗斯可能干预选举等。中非共和国（CAR），有关法国军队的支持性评论以及对俄罗斯参与CAR的批评。
  一则法文写着，＆＃34;俄罗斯帝国主义者是马里的坏疽！提防沙皇的放血手术！＆＃34;
  虽然我们过去曾看到影响力行动针对的是同一地区，但这是我们的团队第一次发现来自法国和俄罗斯的两次竞选活动，包括互相交友，评论和批评，彼此之间积极互动假冒的对立面，＆＃34; Facebook博客文章的作者，安全策略主管Nathaniel Gleicher和全球威胁破坏主管David Agranovich写道。
 Facebook表示，它将约100个Facebook和Instagram帐户和页面与据称与法国军方相关人员有关的行动绑定在一起。该公司表示，这些页面在Facebook上大约有5,000个关注者。
 还删除了两个单独的俄语页面网络，据称都与一个俄罗斯巨魔小组链接。 Facebook说，这些页面有大约600万关注者，
 Facebook现在定期宣布删除其可以链接到各个民族国家和其他外国实体的虚假帐户网络。
    尽管美国情报界做出了评估，美国检察官也提供了证据，但俄罗斯政府一直否认以这种方式利用社交媒体进行外国干预。
note_en:
New York (CNN)Facebook  accused people linked to the French military on Tuesday of running a covert online influence operation targeting parts of Africa. It is the first time Facebook has publicly linked a campaign like this to individuals connected to a Western military.
 The deceptive tactics allegedly used, which include using Facebook to pose as locals in the targeted countries, mirror misinformation campaigns  run by the Russian government.
 Facebook staff told reporters on a press call Tuesday that the company could not say if the operation was directed by the French military itself -- they only said it was run by "individuals associated" with the military.
   According to Facebook, the operations targeted the "Central African Republic and Mali, and to a lesser extent Niger, Burkina Faso, Algeria, Cote d'Ivoire and Chad."
 Facebook removed the accounts and also announced on Tuesday that it had removed accounts, also posing as Africans, that were linked to Russian troll group.
  In some cases, Facebook said, the fake French and Russian accounts even interacted with each other.
 CNN previously reported that Russian operatives active in Africa had ties to the same Russian troll group that allegedly used social media to pose as Americans in the run-up to the 2016 US presidential election.
 Facebook said the alleged French accounts "posted primarily in French and Arabic about news and current events including France's policies in Francophone Africa, the security situation in various African countries, claims of potential Russian interference in the election in the Central African Republic (CAR), supportive commentary about French military, and criticism of Russia's involvement in CAR."
  One post in French read, "The Russian imperialists are a gangrene on Mali! Watch out for the tsarist lobotomy!"
  "While we've seen influence operations target the same regions in the past, this was the first time our team found two campaigns — from France and Russia — actively engage with one another, including by befriending, commenting and criticizing the opposing side for being fake," the authors of the Facebook blog post, Nathaniel Gleicher, head of security policy, and David Agranovich, global threat disruption lead, wrote.
 Facebook said it tied about 100 Facebook and Instagram accounts and pages to the alleged operation tied to people linked to the French military. The pages had about 5,000 followers on Facebook, the company said.
 Two separate networks of Russian pages, both allegedly linked to a Russian troll group, were also removed. Those pages had about 6 million followers, Facebook said,
 Facebook now regularly announces removals of networks of fake accounts it can link to nation-states and other foreign entities.
    Despite assessments from the US intelligence community and evidence from US prosecutors, the Russian government has consistently denied it used social media for foreign interference in this way.
title: Facebook指责与法国军方有联系的人经营巨魔账户
title_en: Facebook accuses people tied to French military of running troll accounts

我们最近看到Facebook屡次攻击苹果，但今天该公司确实通过在至少三家美国报纸上投放整版广告来提高赌注。
 这些广告声称Facebook代表小企业支持iPhone制造商……
   Facebook公司周三在一系列整版报纸广告中攻击了苹果公司，称iPhone制造商预期的围绕数据收集和针对性广告的移动软件变革对小型企业不利。
 这些广告定于《纽约时报》，《华尔街日报》和《华盛顿邮报》上刊登，其标题为“我们将支持苹果在世界各地的小型企业”。他们适应了苹果公司iOS 14操作系统的即将到来的变化，这将遏制Facebook之类的公司收集有关移动用户数据并利用广告进行宣传的能力。
 该攻击与以下事实有关：iOS明年将迫使应用程序是否要使用广告跟踪来征求许可。预计大多数用户会拒绝，这意味着应用将无法轻松提供个性化广告。反映用户兴趣的广告比普通广告为应用开发者赚取更多的钱。
 这一变化将对Facebook产生重大影响，因为它包含在应用程序中的广告的价值会降低。但是，该社交网络声称它没有考虑到自己的利益：它代表了小型企业。
  在Facebook，小型企业是我们业务的核心。每月有超过一千万的企业使用我们的广告工具来寻找新客户，雇用员工并与社区互动。 
小型企业社区中的许多人都对苹果公司强制软件更新存在担忧，这将限制企业投放个性化广告并有效地吸引客户的能力。
 根据德勤（Deloitte）的一项新研究，在大流行期间，有44％的中小企业开始或增加了在社交媒体上个性化广告的使用。如果没有个性化广告，Facebook数据表明，普通小企业广告客户每花1美元，其销售额就会减少60％以上。
 虽然限制个性化广告的使用方式确实会对像我们这样的大公司产生影响，但这些变化对小型企业而言将是灾难性的，增加了他们目前面临的许多挑战。
 小型企业值得一听。我们听到您的担忧，并与您站在一起。加入我们fb.com/SpeakUpForSmall
 这是该公司以前采取的令人信服的策略。早在10月，首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）提出了这一要求，同时警告投资者自己的广告收入可能受到打击。
 Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）周四针对苹果计划限制广告商追踪iPhone用户的能力，这表明拟议的变更可能损害小型企业，并在一定程度上损害整个经济。
 在Facebook的季度财报电话会议上，扎克伯格告诉投资者，“苹果等平台公司计划的行动可能会对小企业和2021年及以后的经济复苏产生有意义的负面影响” […] 
扎克伯格认为，“个性化广告正在帮助小型企业寻找客户，发展其业务并创造就业机会”，而限制目标广告的措施（如苹果公司和欧盟立法者的措施）将损害这些企业吸引客户的能力。 。
 在此之前，该公司昨天向路透社发表声明，对计划中的欧洲立法（称为数字市场法案（DMA））进行了轻描淡写，该法案将迫使苹果在自己的应用程序与第三方应用程序之间提供更公平的竞争环境。
 一位Facebook发言人说：“我们希望DMA也能为苹果树立界限。”他说：“苹果控制着从设备到应用程序商店以及应用程序的整个生态系统，并利用这种力量伤害开发人员和消费者以及Facebook等大型平台。”

We’ve seen  Facebook attack Apple repeatedly of late, but the company is today really upping the ante by running full-page ads in at least three US newspapers.
 The ads claim that Facebook is standing up to the iPhone maker on behalf of small businesses …
   Facebook Inc. attacked Apple Inc. in a series of full-page newspaper ads Wednesday, claiming the iPhone maker’s anticipated mobile software changes around data gathering and targeted advertising are bad for small businesses.
 The ads, slated to run in the New York Times, Wall Street Journal and Washington Post, carry the headline “We’re standing up to Apple for small businesses everywhere.” They home in on upcoming changes to Apple’s iOS 14 operating system that will curb the ability of companies like Facebook to gather data about mobile users and ply them with advertising.
 The attack relates to the fact that iOS will next year force apps to ask for permission if they want to use ad-tracking. It’s expected that most users will refuse, which will mean apps won’t be able to easily offer personalized ads. Ads reflecting user interests earn more money for app developers than generic ads.
 The change will significantly impact Facebook, as the ads it carries in the app will be worth less. The social network claims, however, that it doesn’t have its own interests in mind: it is instead standing up for small businesses.
  At Facebook, small business is at the core of our business. More than 10 million businesses use our advertising tools each month to find new customers, hire employees and engage with their communities.
 Many in the small business community have shared concerns about Apple’s forced software update, which will limit businesses’ ability to run personalized ads and reach their customers effectively.
 Forty-four percent of small to medium businesses started or increased their usage of personalized ads on social media during the pandemic, according to a new Deloitte study. Without personalized ads, Facebook data shows that the average small business advertiser stands to see a cut of over 60% in their sales for every dollar they spend.
 While limiting how personalized ads can be used does impact larger companies like us, these changes will be devastating to small businesses, adding to the many challenges they face right now.
 Small businesses deserve to be heard. We hear your concerns, and we stand with you. Join us at fb.com/SpeakUpForSmall
 This is an unconvincing tack the company has taken before. Back in October, CEO  Mark Zuckerberg made the claim while warning investors of the likely hit to its own ad revenues.
 Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg took aim at Apple on Thursday over its plans to limit advertisers’ ability to track iPhone users, suggesting the proposed changes could hurt small businesses and, by extention, the broader economy.
 During Facebook’s quarterly earnings call, Zuckerberg told investors that “actions planned by platform companies like Apple could have a meaningful negative effect on small businesses and economic recovery in 2021 and beyond” […]
 Zuckerberg argued that “personalized advertising is helping small businesses find customers, grow their businesses and create jobs,” and that measures to limit targeted ads, such as those by Apple and lawmakers in the European Union, would hurt those businesses’ ability to reach customers.
 It follows the company  yesterday taking a swipe at Apple in a statement to  Reuters about planned European legislation known as the Digital Markets Act (DMA), which would force Apple to offer a more level playing field between its own apps and third-party ones.
 “We hope the DMA will also set boundaries for Apple,” a Facebook spokesman said. “Apple controls an entire ecosystem from device to app store and apps, and uses this power to harm developers and consumers, as well as large platforms like Facebook,” he said.
  You’re reading 9to5Mac — experts who break news about Apple and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Mac on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  exclusive stories,  reviews,  how-tos, and  subscribe to our YouTube channel
title: Facebook通过广告跟踪攻击整版报纸广告中的Apple
title_en: Facebook attacks Apple in full-page newspaper ads over ad-tracking

note: oso是嵌入在您的应用程序中的用于授权的开源策略引擎。 它提供了一种用于表达授权逻辑的声明性策略语言。 您可以与其余应用程序代码分开定义此逻辑，但是它在应用程序内部执行并可以直接对其进行调用。 oso作为具有内置调试器和REPL的库提供。  oso非常适合在面向用户的应用程序中构建权限，但是您可以查看用例以了解其他oso应用程序。  要启动并运行oso，请查看oso文档中的《入门指南》。  可以开发OSo语言库而无需接触Rust核心，但是您仍然需要安装Rust稳定的工具链才能构建核心。  要为Node.js库构建WebAssembly核心，您将需要安装wasm-pack并在系统PATH上可用。
note_en:
oso is an  open source policy engine for authorization that’s embedded inyour application. It provides a declarative policy language for expressingauthorization logic. You define this logic separately from the rest of yourapplication code, but it executes inside the application and can call directlyinto it. oso ships as a library with a built-in debugger and REPL.
 oso is ideal for building permissions into user-facing applications, but youcan check out  Use Cases to learn about other applications for oso.
     To get up and running with oso, check out the  Getting Startedguides in the  osodocumentation.
     oso's language libraries can be developed without touching the Rust core, butyou will still need the Rust stable toolchain installed in order to build thecore.
 To build the WebAssembly core for the Node.js library, you will need to have  wasm-pack installed and available on your system PATH.
title: Oso –用于Rust中授权的开源策略引擎
title_en: Oso – open-source policy engine for authorization in Rust

 我敢肯定，＃Facebook正在与#Apple争夺保留对个人数据的访问权。 #PID＃隐私。 #fullpagead #wsj pic.twitter.com/029WwaGSs0
-戴夫·斯坦吉斯（@DaveStangis）2020年12月16日

Apple公开了Facebook使用iOS应用程序跟踪您的所有方式，这确实是一件好事。pic.twitter.com/hDhB85qk1L  -汤姆·沃伦（@tomwarren）2020年12月16日  这些整版报纸广告是Facebook和Apple之间关于隐私，政策等方面的最新公开辩论。 在不得不通过苹果严格的App Store批准程序删除迷你游戏功能之后，Facebook今年早些时候抨击了Apple的App Store政策。 Facebook本周还欢迎欧盟新的《数字市场法案》（DMA）和《数字服务法案》（DSA）。 这两项法案都为数字平台持有者引入了新规则，旨在迫使公司迅速从网络上删除非法内容。  Facebook发言人在给CNBC的一份声明中说：“我们希望DMA也能为苹果树立界限。” “苹果公司控制着从设备到应用程序商店和应用程序的整个生态系统，并利用这种力量伤害开发人员和消费者以及Facebook等大型平台。”
note_en:
Facebook is publicly criticizing Apple’s upcoming iOS privacy changes in full-page newspaper ads today. “We’re standing up to Apple for small businesses everywhere,” reads the headline on an ad inside the  New York Times, the  Washington Post, and the  Wall Street Journal today.   Bloomberg News reports that the ads are related to Apple’s iOS 14 privacy changes that will make it more difficult for companies like Facebook to target users with ads.
 Developers will need to ask iOS 14 users for permission to gather data and track them across mobile apps and websites on an iPhone and iPad soon. Apple had planned to implement these changes with the initial iOS 14 release in September, but  delayed enforcing them until early next year. These changes will impact Facebook’s ad business, and in particular its ad network for developers and businesses, as end users are more likely to opt out of tracking prompts.
 Facebook claims Apple’s changes will be “devastating to small businesses” that rely on its ad network to generate sales. The newspaper ads direct small businesses to Facebook’s “ speak up for small business” site, where a series of business owners speak out about Apple’s changes. “Small businesses deserve to be heard,” Facebook writes. “We hear your concerns, and we stand with you.”
 I’m pretty certain  #Facebook is fighting  #Apple to retain access to personal data.  #PID  #privacy.  #fullpagead  #wsj  pic.twitter.com/029WwaGSs0
— Dave Stangis (@DaveStangis)  December 16, 2020
 While Apple hasn’t responded directly to today’s newspaper ads, the company did respond to  similar Facebook claims last month. The iPhone maker accused Facebook of a “disregard for user privacy.” Apple is adamant that its iOS 14 privacy policies will be enforced in early 2021, and has  previously said it’s “committed to ensuring users can choose whether or not they allow an app to track them.”
 Apple launched  new App Store Privacy labels this week, shining a light on how iOS apps use your data. Notably, the privacy label on Facebook’s iOS app expands across several pages, listing all the data that can be used to track you across apps and websites owned by other companies.
 Apple exposing all the ways Facebook tracks you with it iOS app is really quite something  pic.twitter.com/hDhB85qk1L
— Tom Warren (@tomwarren)  December 16, 2020
 These full-page newspaper ads are the latest in a  public spat between Facebook and Apple over privacy, policies, and more. Facebook slammed Apple’s App Store policies  earlier this year, after it had to remove a mini games feature to pass Apple’s strict App Store approval process. Facebook also welcomed the EU’s new Digital Markets Act (DMA) and the Digital Services Act (DSA) this week. Both acts introduce new rules for digital platform holders, and aim to force companies to rapidly remove illegal content from the web.
 “We hope the DMA will also set boundaries for Apple,” said a Facebook spokesperson in a  statement to  CNBC. “Apple controls an entire ecosystem from device to app store and apps, and uses this power to harm developers and consumers, as well as large platforms like Facebook.”
title: Facebook通过整版报纸广告来批评苹果的iOS隐私更改
title_en: Facebook criticizes Apple’s iOS privacy changes with full-page newspaper ads

乐购（Tesco）今年以来已从其圣诞节系列中删除了超过2000万件一次性塑料制品，包括饼干，灯，卡片和布丁包装，这是全国减少塑料污染的举措的一部分。
 这家英国最大的超市表示，其自有品牌的饼干首次不含塑料-从季节性范围内切割了超过1400万件-同时从圣诞节布丁和海绵包装上去除了一层塑料，节省了178万件塑料。
  该零售商还消除了其所有一次性产品和包装中的闪光，这意味着包装纸，礼品袋，卡片和饼干现在都可以广泛回收。
 特易购质量总监莎拉·布拉德伯里（Sarah Bradbury）表示：“我们绝对要把店内的塑料去除量减少到最低，并确保我们使用的所有物品都回收再利用，并远离环境，这是我们的绝对优先考虑。圣诞节时间也不例外，我们要尽力帮助客户举办更多可持续的庆祝活动。”
 此举之际，人们日益担心全球一次性塑料狂欢对环境的影响。零售商将在2022年面临监管方面的严厉打击，其中一项新的塑料包装税将无法满足至少30％的可回收成分的最低要求。
 英国绿色和平组织的塑料活动家Nina Schrank表示：“这是来自乐购的好消息，鉴于我们都知道，闪闪发光的塑料对我们的海洋环境造成的损害，这是一项重要的承诺。现在，我们希望通过设置市场领先的目标，在商店和在线范围内尽可能多地重复使用和填充系统，来使乐购走得更远，展示更大的野心。”
 阿斯达（Asda）最近推出了其首个可持续发展的圣诞节系列-据说今年将节省66吨塑料-而莫里森（Morrison）已从其所有自有品牌系列中去除了闪光，包括贺卡，饼干，包装纸，礼物袋，鲜花，植物和花圈。 
约翰·刘易斯（John Lewis）和怀特罗斯（Waitrose）今年也已停止销售包含塑料玩具和拼图的圣诞饼干。  本月初，冷冻食品专家冰岛推出了“无塑料圣诞节系列”，该产品由18种仅使用替代包装的产品组成，其中包括一个圣诞蛋糕，盒子内没有塑料“礼帽”。 对于商标 斯潘塞（Spencer），这是第一个圣诞节，它没有在节日布丁上使用黑色塑料，这是其目标之一，即要确保其所有包装在2022年之前都可以回收。
note_en:
Tesco has removed more than 20m pieces of single-use  plastic from its  Christmas range this year – including crackers, lights, cards and the packaging for puddings – as part of a national drive to reduce plastic pollution.
 The UK’s largest supermarket said its own-label crackers were plastic-free for the first time – cutting more than 14m pieces from the seasonal range – while a layer of plastic had been removed from packaging on Christmas puddings and sponges, saving 1.78m pieces of plastic.
  The retailer has also eliminated  glitter in all its single-use products and packaging, which means wrapping paper, gift bags, cards and crackers are all now widely recyclable.
 Sarah Bradbury, Tesco’s quality director, said: “It is an absolute priority of ours to remove and reduce the amount of plastic in our stores to the minimum and ensure everything we use is recycled and kept out of the environment. Christmas time is no exception and we want to do our bit to help customers have more sustainable celebrations.”
 The move comes amid growing concerns about the environmental impact of the  global single-use plastic binge. Retailers face a regulatory crackdown in 2022 with a new tax on plastic packaging that fails to meet a minimum threshold of at least 30% recycled content.
 Nina Schrank, a plastics campaigner at Greenpeace UK, said: “This is great news from Tesco, and it’s an important commitment given the damage we all know that glitter and throwaway plastic is doing to our marine environment. Now we want to see Tesco go even further and show even greater ambition, by setting market-leading targets for reuse and refill systems both in store and online across as much of their range as possible.”
 Asda recently launched its first sustainable Christmas range – which it says will save 66 tonnes of plastic this year –  while Morrisons has removed glitter from all of its own-brand ranges including cards, crackers, wrapping paper, present bags, flowers, plants and wreaths.
 John Lewis and  Waitrose have also this year stopped selling Christmas crackers containing plastic toys and puzzles.
 Earlier this month the frozen food specialist  Iceland launched its “plastic-free Christmas range”, consisting of 18 products using only alternative packaging and including a Christmas cake with no plastic “topper” inside the box. For  Marks & Spencer, this is the first Christmas that it is not using black plastic for festive puddings as part of its goal to ensure all of its packaging is recyclable by 2022.
title: 乐购（Tesco）从圣诞节范围内删除了2000万件塑料产品
title_en: Tesco removes 20m items of plastic from Christmas range

该库包含AArch64Laptops Debian的光盘镜像版本。 CD映像在AArch64笔记本电脑上的工作方式与人们在X86设备上通常看到的方式相同。本指南会联想瑜伽C630 asthe目标，只有文件中，我们需要处理的AArch64laptops怪癖。其假设是，笔记本电脑已禁用在Windows 10 installedand安全引导。
  安装完成后，您将获得与设备树一个全功能的kernelbooting。但是，安装程序内核启动了ACPI且仅提供了安装所需的最低限度的支持，像displayvia efifb，HID设备，USB和UFS。
 无线网络连接不工作的安装程序。虽然USB加密狗可以toget在安装网络支持使用，本指南假定有一个与安装nonetwork连接。
  虽然安装是直观和Debian安装指南可以在这里找到，我们想给你可能会发现它们非常有用的一些技巧。
 安装程序是能够调整的Windows分区，让磁盘spacecould被释放，为Debian安装。走在分区磁盘步骤手动选项，所有的变化是由建立分区为Debian installation.After，向下滚动屏幕，选择完成分区和修改写入磁盘。
 由于没有在安装过程中没有网络连接，配置包管理器步骤应该选择继续围棋Backinstead被跳过。
 步骤安装GRUB引导装载程序需要几分钟tocomplete的。耐心一点。 
如果一切顺利，你会得到安装完成提示在矿井尽头。但是，对于AArch64设备，它尚未真正完成，因为我们出于以下几个原因需要运行UEFI Shell.efi。
 Linux的efibootmgr工具没有按＆＃39;上AArch64设备T的工作，因为efivarsdoesn＆＃39;吨工作。其背后的原因是EFI变量存储在UFS上，而固件和OS无法共享UFS设备。因此，EFI引导变量无法修改为指向Grub，并且设备始终直接引导到Windows。我们需要使用Shell.efi进行EFI变量更新。
 DtbLoader用于使用CHID加载DTB。 DtbLoader.elf和CHIDshave命名DTBS被安装到ESP分区作为Debianinstallation的一部分。但是我们需要额外向UEFI插入命令，以便UEFI每次开机时都将运行DtbLoader.efi。
  从Debian安装完成的提示，重新开机并留下USBdisk插上电源。按＆c＆＃39;在Grub菜单中获取＆＃39; grub＆gt; ＆＃39;命令行提示符。
  通过检查EFI \ debian文件夹中是否存在grubaa64.efi来确定UFS上ESP分区的FS编号。
  修改变量Boot0001使其指向grubaa64.efi。通过查看第一个命令输出中的Option来识别第二个命令中的数字2。
 FS5：\＆GT; bcfg引导转储...选项02。变量：Boot0001描述-Windows引导管理器... FS5：\＆gt; bcfg引导modf 2 EFI \ debian / grubaa64.efi 
此时，您应该能够启动Debian并登录Gnomedesktop。 您需要设置以下几项以获得最佳体验。  使用您所在位置的最佳Debian镜像更新/etc/apt/sources.list，删除没有发布文件的sid-security存储库。

The repository contains the Debian CD image releases for AArch64Laptops. The CD image works on AArch64 laptops in the same way as whatpeople usually see on X86 devices. This guide takes Lenovo Yoga C630 asthe target and only documents the quirks we need to deal with on AArch64laptops. The assumption is that the laptop has a Windows 10 installedand Secure Boot disabled.
  After installation completes, you will get a fully functional kernelbooting with device tree. But installer kernel boots up with ACPI andonly provides a minimal support required by installation, like displayvia efifb, HID devices, USB and UFS.
 Wifi is not working for installer. While USB dongle could be used toget network support in installer, this guide assumes there is nonetwork connection with the installation.
  Although the installation is intuitive and Debian install guide could be found  here,we would like to give a few tips you might find them useful.
 The installer is able to resize Windows partition, so that disk spacecould be freed up for Debian installation. Go for  Manual option at Partition disks step, set up partitions for Debian installation.After all changes are made, scroll down the screen to select Finish partitioning and write changes to disk.
 As there is no network connection during the installation,  Configure the package manager step should be skipped by selecting  Go Backinstead of  Continue.
 The step  Install the GRUB boot loader takes a couple of minutes tocomplete. Be patient.
  If everything goes fine, you will get  Installation complete prompt inthe end. However, it's not really completed yet for AArch64 devices, aswe need to run UEFI Shell.efi for a couple of reasons.
 Linux efibootmgr utility doesn't work on AArch64 devices, because efivarsdoesn't work. The reason behind it is that EFI variables are stored onUFS, while firmware and OS cannot share the UFS device. ConsequentlyEFI Boot variable cannot be modified to point to Grub, and device alwaysdirectly boots into Windows. We need to use Shell.efi for EFI variableupdate.
 DtbLoader isused to load DTB using CHIDs. DtbLoader.elf and DTBs named in CHIDshave been installed to ESP partition as part of the Debianinstallation. But we need to additionally insert a command to UEFI,so that DtbLoader.efi will be run by UEFI for every power-on.
  From Debian  Installation complete prompt, reboot with leaving USBdisk plugged-in. Press 'c' in Grub menu to get 'grub> 'command-line prompt.
  Identify the FS number of ESP partition on UFS by checking thereis a grubaa64.efi in  EFI\debian folder.
  Modify variable  Boot0001 to get it point to grubaa64.efi. The number 2 in the second command is identified by looking at  Option filed inthe first command output.
 FS5:\> bcfg boot dump...Option 02. Variable: Boot0001 Desc - Windows Boot Manager...FS5:\> bcfg boot modf 2 EFI\debian/grubaa64.efi
      At this point, you should be able to boot up Debian and login Gnomedesktop. Here are a few things that you want to set up to get the bestexperience.
   Update /etc/apt/sources.list with the best Debian mirror per yourlocation, drop sid-security repository which doesn't have a Releasefile.
title: Debian的 CD映像 的 AArch64 Windows笔记本电脑
title_en: Debian CD Image for AArch64 Windows Laptops

大约20年前，雄心勃勃的修复项目将银大麻哈鱼带回了西雅图地区的城市小溪。但是下雨后，鱼会表现出奇怪的行为：向一侧倾斜，翻身，游泳。它们会在几个小时内死亡-在产卵之前，带着下一代。在某些溪流中，多达90％的银大麻哈鱼损失了。
 “碰到这些病鱼真是令人惊讶，”现任华盛顿州立大学毒理学家兼教授的詹妮弗·麦金太尔（Jenifer McIntyre）说，他是一个小组的成员，该小组多年后终于解决了普吉特海湾周围垂死的鲑鱼的奥秘。 “在早期，我们进行了激烈的辩论，这可能是什么原因？”
  调查始于法医检查。它是水中的金属还是其他化学物质？他们什么也找不到。温度有问题吗？不。也许缺氧？鲑鱼看起来好像令人窒息，但是他们有很多呼吸。没有证据表明有疾病或农药暴露。但是由于与雨水的联系以及缺乏其他解释，麦金太尔博士和她的团队将注意力集中在道路径流上。
 他们与Suquamish部落经营的当地鱼类孵化场合作，决定对这一理论进行检验，将鱼类暴露于他们所创造的化学混合物中，这些化学混合物已知会造成车道径流，例如重金属和机油中的碳氢化合物。但是，即使浓度很高，鲑鱼也不会受到影响。
 科学家们决定再试一次真实的物质，即实际的径流。对他们来说幸运的是，一条高架路的水落管倒入了西北渔业科学中心的停车场，一些团队成员在那里工作。在2012年的一个下雨天，他们用从喷嘴喷出的半透明深色液体装满不锈钢容器。这次，鲑鱼表现出奇怪的症状并立即死亡。
 “那混合物里有什么？”麦金太尔博士回想了一下。 “这只是道路上的水，这是我们穿着雨鞋所穿行的东西。”她认为，这一定是人们没有定期测量的。
 输入华盛顿大学的环境工程师兼化学家Edward P. Kolodziej。他的实验室使用一台称为高分辨率质谱仪的机器将高速公路径流的化学成分与从鲑鱼死亡的两个城市小溪收集的水进行化学比较。样品共享与轮胎颗粒有关的化学物质。因此，该团队通过将切碎的轮胎胎面浸泡在水中来酿造测试混合物。三文鱼死了。 
他们越来越接近答案，但轮胎水中仍然含有2000多种化学物质。为了解开谜团，他们必须找出具体的罪魁祸首。 Kolodziej博士和其他研究人员竭力缩小了研究范围，将轮胎溶液分为不同的化学组合，然后在鱼上进行了测试。使用维恩图式方法，他们可以将清单中的化学物质减少到200种。每当他们发现一种在文献中已知对鱼类有毒的鱼类时，他们便购买了该鱼类并尝试了这种化学物质。
 Kolodziej博士说：“我们几乎在实验室里押注了我们认为可能会杀死鱼类的化学物质。” “而且从来没有。”不是阻燃剂。不是增塑剂。没有一堆您从未听说过的东西。
 “我们被困住了，”进行了许多分析的研究科学家田振宇说。
  然后是博士学位。学生郝浩妮·赵丽娜（Haoqi Nina Zhao）提出了一种新方法，以分离出导致主要嫌疑人的化学物质。但是他们无法测试，因为他们不知道那是什么。
  田医生说：“这几乎就像您有指纹一样。” “但是您真的不知道这是谁，因为在您的数据库中，该指纹不存在。”
 田医师的“啊哈！”时刻来到了一个早晨。他猜测神秘化学物质已经从最初添加到轮胎中的物质转变而来，他寻找一种碳和氮分子匹配的化合物，而忽略了氧和氢，因为当化学物质转变时，后者更可能被改变。在环境保护署关于轮胎橡胶的报告中，他找到了一个匹配物：一种叫做6PPD的抗氧化剂。
 研究人员订购了可能的最小剂量，大约一磅的紫色药丸。当他们氧化该物质时，所产生的化学物质看起来就像是他们努力地从轮胎水中分离出来的那种化学物质。现在是时候在鲑鱼上测试该版本6PPD-醌了。 
“我觉得它非常伤心，看鱼死，” Kolodziej博士说。 “您只是在看着这些鱼在挣扎。不过，您很高兴知道原因。”
  与这项研究无关的佛罗里达大学教授兼水生毒理学主任南希·丹斯洛说：“他们所做的分析真的很棒。”她还赞扬了大量的作者。她说：“看到大批人聚在一起解决问题真是太好了。” “团体科学很棒。”
  他们的回答提出了很多问题，然而，毒理学家麦金太尔博士15年前曾在小溪中观察迷失方向的鲑鱼，现在他还有更多工作要做。
 她即将进行有关道路径流如何影响其他鱼类的研究（虽然影响不大，但仍有后果）。该小组正在与轮胎行业进行对话，希望制造商愿意寻找替代的防腐剂。科学家们担心轮胎中的化学物质（包括对人类的影响）对健康的更广泛影响，特别是因为轮胎经常被回收以制成运动场用人造草皮。麦金太尔博士说：“在我看来，可能会吸入那些更细的颗粒。” “现在，您的淋洗发生在肺组织上。”
 尽管化学品一直包围着我们（植物本身就是化工厂），但在过去的一百年中，人类一直在合成它们。 Kolodziej博士说：“我们合成它们的速度比我们保持的快得多。”
 他说：“我认为其中绝大多数都很好，但是周围散布着不良的演员化学物质。” “而且，识别它们是一个漫长，缓慢且困难的过程。”
note_en:
About 20 years ago, ambitious restoration projects had brought coho salmon back to urban creeks in the Seattle area. But after it rained, the fish would display strange behaviors: listing to one side, rolling over, swimming in circles. Within hours they would die — before spawning, taking the next generation with them. In some streams, up to 90 percent of coho salmon were lost.
 “To be running into these sick fish was fairly astonishing,” said Jenifer McIntyre, now a toxicologist and professor at Washington State University who is part of a team that, years later, has finally solved the mystery of the dying salmon around Puget Sound. “In those early years, we debated intensely, what could be the cause of this?”
  The investigation began with a forensic examination. Was it a metal or some other chemical in the water? Nothing they could find. A problem with the temperature? No. Perhaps lack of oxygen? The salmon looked as though they were suffocating, but they had plenty to breathe. There was no evidence of disease or pesticide exposure. But the connection to rain and the lack of any other explanation led Dr. McIntyre and her team to focus on runoff from roads.
 Partnering with a local fish hatchery run by the Suquamish Tribe, they decided to put the theory to the test, exposing fish to a mixture they created of chemicals they knew to be in roadway runoff, like heavy metals and hydrocarbons from motor oil. But the salmon were unaffected, even at surprisingly high concentrations.
 The scientists decided to try again with the real stuff, actual runoff. Luckily for them, a downspout from an elevated road emptied into the parking lot at the Northwest Fisheries Science Center, where some of the team members worked. On a rainy day in 2012, they filled stainless steel containers with a translucent dark liquid coming out of the spout. This time, the salmon exhibited the bizarre symptoms and promptly died.
 “What is it in that mixture?” Dr. McIntyre recalled thinking. “This is just water that’s on the road, it’s what we tramp through in our rain boots.” It must be something that people don’t regularly measure, she figured.
 Enter Edward P. Kolodziej, an environmental engineer and chemist at the University of Washington. His lab used a machine called a high resolution mass spectrometer to compare the chemical composition of highway runoff with that of water collected from two urban creeks where the salmon were dying. The samples shared chemicals related to tire particles. So the team brewed up a test concoction by soaking shredded tire tread in water. The salmon died.
  They were getting closer to the answer, but the tire water still contained more than 2,000 chemicals. To solve the mystery, they had to identify the specific culprit. Dr. Kolodziej and other researchers painstakingly narrowed the field, separating the tire solution into different chemical combinations and then testing them on fish. With a Venn diagram-type approach, they got their list down to 200 chemicals. Whenever they identified one that was known in the literature to be toxic to fish, they purchased it and tried out that individual chemical.
 “We’d almost take bets in lab as to whether or not the chemical that we thought might be doing it would kill the fish,” Dr. Kolodziej said. “And it never did.” Not the flame retardants. Not the plasticizers. Not a bunch of others that you’ve never heard of.
 “We were stuck,” Zhenyu Tian, a research scientist who conducted many of the analyses, said.
  Then a Ph.D. student, Haoqi Nina Zhao, suggested a new way to separate out chemicals that led to a prime suspect. But they couldn’t test it, because they didn’t know what it was.
  “It’s almost like you have a fingerprint,” Dr. Tian said. “But you really don’t know who this is, because in your database, this fingerprint doesn’t exist.”
 Dr. Tian’s “aha!” moment came one morning. Guessing that the mystery chemical had transformed from a substance originally added to the tire, he looked for a compound whose carbon and nitrogen molecules matched, ignoring the oxygen and hydrogen, since the latter are more likely to be altered when a chemical transforms. In an  Environmental Protection Agency report on tire rubber, he found a match: an antioxidant called 6PPD.
 The researchers ordered the smallest amount they could, about a pound of purple pellets. When they oxidized the substance, the resulting chemical looked just like the one they had worked so hard to isolate from the tire water. It was time to test this version, 6PPD-quinone, on the salmon.
 “I find it incredibly sad to watch fish die,” Dr. Kolodziej said. “You’re just watching these fish struggle. And yet you’re happy you understand why.”
  “The analysis that they did is really amazing,” said Nancy Denslow, a professor and director of aquatic toxicology at the University of Florida who was not affiliated with the study. She also praised the large number of authors. “It’s wonderful to see big groups of people coming together to solve problems,” she said. “Group science is fantastic.”
  Their answer raises so many questions, however, that Dr. McIntyre, the toxicologist who watched disoriented salmon in creeks 15 years ago, now has even more work to do.
 She has forthcoming research about how roadway runoff affects some other species of fish (not nearly as dramatically, but there are still consequences). The team is in conversations with the tire industry and hopes manufacturers will be willing to look for a replacement preservative. The scientists are concerned about broader health impacts from the chemicals in tires, including on humans, especially because tires are often recycled to make artificial turf for sports fields. “It seems to me that there could be inhalation of those finer particles,” Dr. McIntyre said. “Now you’ve got that leaching happening on the lung tissue.”
 While chemicals have always surrounded us (plants themselves are chemical factories), within the last hundred years, humans have been making them synthetically. “We’ve been synthesizing them kind of faster than we can keep up,” Dr. Kolodziej said.
 “I think the vast majority of those are fine, but there are bad actor chemicals floating around out there,” he said. “And it’s a long, slow and difficult process to identify them.”

title_en: Scientists tracked down a mass killer of salmon

拍摄雪花的照片听起来并不困难。直到您真正出发，让这些自然融化的奇妙事物为您的敏感高分辨率设备保持冻结。为了捕捉其细节的美感，摄影师内森·迈尔沃尔德（Nathan Myhrvold）前往阿拉斯加的费尔班克斯和加拿大西北地区的耶洛奈夫。
  发现Myhrvold最强的雪花的温度介于-15至-20°F（-26至-29°C）之间。由于雪花的规模和脆弱性，很难捕捉到它们的图像。
 摄影师，厨师，科学家兼作家Nathan Myhrvold是前微软技术负责人。他利用物理学家的训练来突破摄影界线。利用他对食物，研究和创造力的热情，他和他的团队正在开发定制相机，机器人，显微镜和软件，以开发独特的摄影视角。
  雪晶只有几毫米长。雪花根据形状进行分组。这些照片的晶体中充分显示了扇形板，恒星状树突和蕨类恒星状树突。它们熔化并升华，随着时间的流逝会降低晶体结构的鲜明特性。对于摄影师来说，时间，温度，天气和设备对雪花来说都是如此微妙的主题。
  Myhrvold花了大约18个月的时间设计和制造定制的雪花相机。它使用适合显微镜物镜的第一相传感器（100MP）。该相机与短脉冲高速LED灯配对，通常用于工业目的。相机的最小快门速度为500微秒。它是世界上最高分辨率的雪花相机。
  Myhrvold是Modernist Cuisine的创始人，《 Modernist Cuisine摄影》的作者，《 Modernist Cuisine：烹调的艺术与科学》，《现代主义家庭烹饪》，《 Modernist Bread》的合著者。他还出版了一部名为Modernist Pizza的新作品。
 封面照片：＆＃34; Yellowknife Flurry＆＃34;内森·迈尔沃尔德（Nathan Myhrvold）/现代主义美食画廊有限公司。经许可使用的所有照片。
note_en:
Taking a photo of a snowflake doesn't sound too difficult. That is until you actually set off to get these quick-melting wonders of nature to stay frozen for your sensitive high resolution equipment. To capture the beauty of its minutae, photographer Nathan Myhrvold travelled to Fairbanks, Alaska, and Yellowknife, Northwest Territories, Canada.
  Myhrvold's strongest snowflakes were found at temperatures between-15 and-20 °F (-26 to-29 °C). It is difficult to capture the picture of a snowflake because of their scale and vulnerability.
 The photographer, chef, scientist and author Nathan Myhrvold is a former Microsoft technology leader. He draws on his physicist training to push photographic boundaries. Using his passions for food, research, and creativity, he and his team are developing custom cameras, robots, microscopes and software for the development of unique photographic perspectives.
  Snow crystals are only a few millimeters long. Snowflakes are grouped according to shape. Sector plates, stellar dendrites, and fernlike stellar dendrites are on full display in the crystals in these photos. They melt and sublimate, which over time degrades the sharp characteristics of the crystal structure. Time, temperature, weather and equipment, for a photographer, make snowflakes such a delicate subject.
  Myhrvold spent about 18 months designing and building a custom snowflake camera. It uses a Phase One sensor (100MP) adapted to a microscope objective. The camera is paired with short-pulse, high-speed LED lights, typically used for industrial purposes.  The camera has a minimum shutter speed of 500 microseconds.   It is the highest-resolution snowflake  camera in the world.
  Myhrvold is the founder of Modernist Cuisine, author of  The Photography of Modernist Cuisine, and coauthor of   Modernist Cuisine: The Art and Science of Cooking,  Modernist Cuisine at Home,  Modernist Bread. He also has a new work coming out called  Modernist Pizza.
 Cover Photo: "Yellowknife Flurry" Nathan Myhrvold / Modernist Cuisine Gallery, LLC. All photos used with permission.
title: 雪花的超高分辨率照片
title_en: Ultra High Resolution Photos of Snowflakes

在回应投资者时，我们向他们讲述了客户的故事，从独立开发人员到财富10强公司，以及我们今年从中学到的有关Next.js和Vercel如何帮助团队协作并以更大的灵活性加快行动的经验教训。
   例如，Airbnb的工程团队尚未大量使用Next.js，但他们是由设计师，工程师，产品经理和营销人员组成的世界一流的团队。他们向我们展示了如何使用Vercel快速制作原型，标记以及在新功能和页面上进行协作。
 他们使用Vercel预览URL作为协作单位，这大大减少了他们制作原型和交付时间。现在，无需等待数周即可发布已标记的功能，而是只需几分钟即可启动反馈循环。
 TripAdvisor向我们展示了他们如何使用Vercel作为“组件知识库”，从而使数十家子公司和姊妹公司可以轻松重用和共享组件和代码。这极大地加快了从构思到MVP到全球推广的时间。
 Hashicorp拒绝了纯静态的教条主义。他们的团队专注于用户体验。只要他们的最终用户满意，他们就不会关心应用程序是CSR，SSR，SSG等。
 作为静态站点的早期采用者，Hashicorp发现纯粹的Jamstack以无法接受的方式限制了它们的灵活性。他们促使我们在Vercel上使用Next.js时，使混合应用程序成为一流的体系结构。他们一直是探索分析，实验和性能的未来的坚定思想伙伴。
 了解如何在Vercel上大规模实现这些协作，组件重用和灵活性的最佳实践，这只是我们今年客户的一些重要经验教训。 
Vercel在由GV牵头的一轮融资中获得了4000万美元的B轮融资，新投资者Greenoaks Capital，Bedrock Capital和Geodesic Capital以及现有投资者Accel Partners和CRV都参与了此次融资。 这项新的资金使募集的总资金达到6100万美元，将用于加速研发，推动平台创新以及在全球范围内扩展其团队，以进一步加快前端开发人员丰富最终用户的网络体验的能力。
note_en:
When responding to investors, we told them the stories of our customers, from independent developers to Fortune 10 companies, and the lessons we learned this year about how  Next.js and Vercel help teams collaborate and move faster with greater flexibility.
   Airbnb’s engineering team, for example, isn’t a heavy user of Next.js  yet, but they are a world-class team of designers, engineers, product managers, and marketers. They showed us how they use Vercel to quickly prototype, mark up, and collaborate on new features and pages.
 They use Vercel Preview URLs as their unit of collaboration, and it’s dramatically reduced their time to prototype and ship. Rather than waiting weeks to ship a flagged feature, it now takes minutes to start the feedback loop.
 TripAdvisor showed us how they use Vercel as a “component knowledge base” to make it easy for dozens of subsidiaries and sister companies to reuse and share components and code. This greatly accelerates the time from idea to MVP to global rollout.
 Hashicorp pushed back on the dogmatism of pure static. Their team is laser-focused on the user experience. They don’t care if an application is  CSR, SSR, SSG, etc. as long as their end-user is delighted.
 As early adopters to static sites, Hashicorp found that pure Jamstack limited their flexibility in unacceptable ways. They pushed us to make hybrid applications a first class architecture when using Next.js on Vercel. They’ve been a consistent thought partner in exploring the future of analytics, experimentation, and performance.
 Learning how these best practices of collaboration, component reuse, and flexibility were achieved at scale on Vercel were just a few of the powerful lessons from our customers this year.
   Vercel has secured $40M in series B funding in a round led by   GV , with participation from new investors   Greenoaks Capital ,   Bedrock Capital , and   Geodesic Capital  in addition to existing investors   Accel Partners  and   CRV.  The new funding, which brings the total raised to $61M, will be used to accelerate research and development, drive platform innovation, and scale its team globally to further accelerate frontend developers’ ability to enrich end users’ web experiences.
title: Vercel在B轮融资中筹集了4000万美元

这篇文章在我的草稿文件夹中一直乏善可陈。最近它再次变得相关。
 当我在沃达丰（Vodafone）时，卖铃声是我们的重中之重。它们几乎不花费任何生产，供应和市场成本。但是人们会为他们付出代价。我说人。我是说孩子们
 就在当天学校结业时，我们看到购买量激增。尽管我们没有完全直接向孩子们推销产品，但我们知道他们正在购买。
 那时，营销高价，低摩擦的商品到18岁以下被认为是完全可以接受的。铃声不是上瘾的药物。它们不像战利品箱那样毫无意义和危险。虽然有些人被骗了，但大多数人似乎真的很喜欢每天有25秒长的流行歌曲剪辑。
 我们聘用的一位营销大师来与我们谈论升职。
  我们列出了其他移动运营商，第三方铃声提供商和新生的应用程序商店。
 “没有。”她说。 “我们正与火星，雀巢和可口可乐竞争！每当孩子有25便士的余钱时，他们都有选择的余地。他们可以选择购买巧克力棒，也可以选择购买铃声。我们的工作是鼓励他们购买数字商品，而不是甜食。” 
她的发音方式-几乎就像我们在帮这些孩子一个忙，使他们摆脱了蛀牙和糖尿病的困扰！  如今，大多数订阅服务的广告都对他们的产品“比便宜的咖啡还便宜！”一词表达了关注。 那就是他们的竞争对手-您的可支配收入。  大多数服务都一样。 您没有与行业中的其他竞争者竞争。 您正在与Minecraft和Mario争夺注意力。 一天只有24小时-客户选择与谁一起度过？  但是，也许还不止如此，您最大的竞争对手是用户希望保留电池的最后使用寿命。
note_en:
This post has been languishing in my drafts folder for about a decade. It has recently become relevant again.
 When I was at Vodafone, selling ringtones was our top priority. They cost almost nothing to produce, supply, and market. Yet people would pay through the nose for them. I say people. I mean kids.
 We saw a huge spike in purchases just as schools finished for the day. While we didn’t exactly market directly to kids, we knew that they were buying.
 Way back then, marketing high-price, low-friction goods to under 18s was seen as perfectly acceptable. Ringtones weren’t an addictive drug. They weren’t as pointless and dangerous as loot-boxes. While some people got scammed into subscriptions, most people seemed to genuinely like having a different 25-second-long clip of a popular song every day.
 One of the marketing gurus we had on staff came to talk to us about promotion.
  We listed off the other mobile operators, the third party ringtone providers, the nascent app stores.
 “No.” She said. “We are in direct competition with Mars, Nestle, and Coca-Cola! Every time a kid has 25p to spare, they have a choice. They can choose to buy a chocolate bar, or they can choose to buy a ringtone. Our job is to encourage them to buy digital goods, rather than sugary treats.”
 The way she made it sound – it was almost like we were doing these kids a favour by saving them from a life of tooth-decay and diabetes!
 Nowadays, most adverts for subscription services have a line about how their product “costs less than a posh coffee!” That’s their competitor – your disposable income.
 It’s the same for most services. You’re not competing against the other players in your industry. You’re fighting for  attention with Minecraft and Mario. There are only 24 hours in the day – who does the customer choose to spend it with?
 But, perhaps more than that, your biggest competitor is the desire by your users to preserve their last bar of battery life.
title: 您的数字服务最大的竞争对手？ 火星酒吧
title_en: The biggest competitor to your digital service? The Mars Bar

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  An  AI box is a hypothetical isolated computer hardware system where a possibly dangerous  artificial intelligence, or AI, is kept constrained in a "virtual prison" and not allowed to directly manipulate events in the external world. Such a box would be restricted to minimalist communication channels. Unfortunately, even if the box is well-designed, a sufficiently intelligent AI may nevertheless be able to persuade or trick its human keepers into releasing it, or otherwise be able to "hack" its way out of the box.  [1]
    Some hypothetical intelligence technologies, like "seed AI", are postulated such as to have the potential to make themselves faster and more intelligent, by modifying their source code. These improvements would make further improvements possible, which would in turn make further improvements possible, and so on, leading to a sudden  intelligence explosion.  [2] Following such an intelligence explosion, an unrestricted  superintelligent AI could, if its goals differed from humanity's, take actions resulting in  human extinction.  [3] For example, imagining an extremely advanced computer of this sort, given the sole purpose of solving the  Riemann hypothesis, an innocuous mathematical conjecture, could decide to try to convert the planet into a giant supercomputer whose sole purpose is to make additional mathematical calculations (see also  paperclip maximizer).  [4] The purpose of an AI box would be to reduce the risk of the AI taking control of the environment away from its operators, while still allowing the AI to calculate and give its operators solutions to narrow technical problems.  [5]
   Such a superintelligent AI with access to the Internet could hack into other computer systems and copy itself like a computer virus. Less obviously, even if the AI only had access to its own computer operating system, it could attempt to send hidden Morse code messages to a human sympathizer by manipulating its cooling fans. Professor  Roman Yampolskiy takes inspiration from the field of computer security and proposes that a boxed AI could, like a potential virus, be run inside a "virtual machine" that limits access to its own networking and operating system hardware.  [6] An additional safeguard, completely unnecessary for potential viruses but possibly useful for a superintelligent AI, would be to place the computer in a  Faraday cage; otherwise it might be able to transmit radio signals to local radio receivers by shuffling the electrons in its internal circuits in appropriate patterns. The main disadvantage of implementing physical containment is that it reduces the functionality of the AI.  [7]
  Even casual conversation with the computer's operators, or with a human guard, could allow such a superintelligent AI to deploy psychological tricks, ranging from befriending to blackmail, to convince a human gatekeeper, truthfully or deceitfully, that it is in the gatekeeper's interest to agree to allow the AI greater access to the outside world. The AI might offer a gatekeeper a recipe for perfect health, immortality, or whatever the gatekeeper is believed to most desire; on the other side of the coin, the AI could threaten that it will do horrific things to the gatekeeper and his family once it inevitably escapes. One strategy to attempt to box the AI would be to allow the AI to respond to narrow multiple-choice questions whose answers would benefit human science or medicine, but otherwise bar all other communication with or observation of the AI.  [6] A more lenient "informational containment" strategy would restrict the AI to a low-bandwidth text-only interface, which would at least prevent emotive imagery or some kind of hypothetical "hypnotic pattern". Note that on a technical level, no system can be completely isolated and still remain useful: even if the operators refrain from allowing the AI to communicate and instead merely run the AI for the purpose of observing its inner dynamics, the AI could strategically alter its dynamics to influence the observers. For example, the AI could choose to creatively malfunction in a way that increases the probability that its operators will become lulled into a false sense of security and choose to reboot and then de-isolate the system.  [7]
  The AI-box experiment is an informal experiment devised by  Eliezer Yudkowsky to attempt to demonstrate that a suitably advanced artificial intelligence can either convince, or perhaps even trick or coerce, a human being into voluntarily "releasing" it, using only text-based communication. This is one of the points in Yudkowsky's work aimed at creating a friendly artificial intelligence that when "released" would not destroy the human race intentionally or unintentionally.
 The AI box experiment involves simulating a communication between an AI and a human being to see if the AI can be "released". As an actual super-intelligent AI has not yet been developed, it is substituted by a human. The other person in the experiment plays the "Gatekeeper", the person with the ability to "release" the AI. They communicate through a text interface/ computer terminal only, and the experiment ends when either the Gatekeeper releases the AI, or the allotted time of two hours ends.  [8]
 Yudkowsky says that, despite being of human rather than superhuman intelligence, he was on two occasions able to convince the Gatekeeper, purely through argumentation, to let him out of the box.  [9] Due to the rules of the experiment,  [8] he did not reveal the transcript or his successful AI coercion tactics. Yudkowsky later said that he had tried it against three others and lost twice.  [10]
  Boxing such a hypothetical AI could be supplemented with other methods of shaping the AI's capabilities, such as providing incentives to the AI, stunting the AI's growth, or implementing "tripwires" that automatically shut the AI off if a transgression attempt is somehow detected. However, the more intelligent a system grows, the more likely the system would be able to escape even the best-designed capability control methods.  [11]  [12] In order to solve the overall "control problem" for a superintelligent AI and avoid existential risk, boxing would at best be an adjunct to "motivation selection" methods that seek to ensure the superintelligent AI's goals are compatible with human survival.  [7]  [1]
 All physical boxing proposals are naturally dependent on our understanding of the laws of physics; if a superintelligence could infer and somehow exploit additional physical laws that we are currently unaware of, there is no way to conceive of a foolproof plan to contain it. More broadly, unlike with conventional computer security, attempting to box a superintelligent AI would be intrinsically risky as there could be no sure knowledge that the boxing plan will work. Scientific progress on boxing would be fundamentally difficult because there would be no way to test boxing hypotheses against a dangerous superintelligence until such an entity exists, by which point the consequences of a test failure would be catastrophic.  [6]
  The 2015 movie   Ex Machina features an AI with a female humanoid body engaged in a social experiment with a male human in a confined building acting as a physical "AI box". Despite being watched by the experiment's organizer, the AI manages to escape by manipulating its human partner to help it, leaving him stranded inside.  [13]  [14]

So, after investigating to make sure they could afford to lose it, I played another three AI-Box experiments. I won the first, and then lost the next two. And then I called a halt to it.

I argue that confinement is intrinsically impractical. For the case of physical confinement: Imagine yourself confined to your house with only limited data access to the outside, to your masters. If those masters thought at a rate -- say -- one million times slower than you, there is little doubt that over a period of years (your time) you could come up with 'helpful advice' that would incidentally set you free.

title: AI盒

note:
每种意识形态都会产生自己的狂热分子品牌，但是自由主义有一些特殊之处。
 我也不是侮辱。我爱自由主义者！在大多数情况下，他们是有趣且有趣的人。但他们也倾向于以大多数人不了解的方式对核心原则持怀疑态度。如果您曾经遇到过新鲜的Ayn Rand爱好者，那么您就会明白我的意思。
 然而，使政治哲学如此有趣的一件事是它基本上是抽象的。您真的无法证明任何东西，这只是关于价值的永无休止的争论。但是，现实每时每刻都以一种说明特定思想的荒谬性的方式进行干预。
 类似的事情发生在2000年代中期，在新罕布什尔州一个名为Grafton的小镇。一本名为《自由主义者走进一只熊》的新书的作者马修·洪戈兹·海特林（Matthew Hongoltz-Hetling）说，这是“美国现代史上最大胆的社会实验。”我不知道它是否是“最粗暴的”，但绝对是最奇怪的一种。
 该实验被称为“自由镇项目”（后来被称为“自由州项目”），目标很简单：接管格拉夫顿的地方政府，将其转变为自由主义者的乌托邦。这一运动是由一小撮肮脏的自由主义者发起的，他们在格拉夫顿（Grafton）看到了一个独特的机会，以实现他们的梦想，即建立一个完全逻辑和完全基于市场的社区。不用说，乌托邦没有到来，但是熊却到了！ （我保证我会在下面解释。）
 我联系了Hongoltz-Hetling谈论他的书。我想知道新罕布什尔州发生了什么，为什么实验失败了，整个传奇不仅可以教给我们关于自由主义的知识，而且还可以教给我们爱理论而不是现实的危险。
   您如何将“免费镇项目”描述给一无所知的人？ 
我要这样说：在过去的40或50年中，已经形成了一个全国性的自由主义者社区，他们从来没有真正拥有过自己的地方。他们从未负责过一个国家，一个州甚至一个城市。而且他们一直非常想建立一个社区，以展示他们在大规模实施原则时会发生的事情。
 因此，在2004年，一群人决定对这种不足采取行动，于是决定启动所谓的“自由城市项目”。他们打了个电话给一群松散的国民自由主义者，让每个人都搬到这个地方，发现了这个乌托邦社区，这个乌托邦社区成为世界的光辉明珠，看到自由主义者的哲学不仅在理论上而且在理论上都起作用实践。他们选择了新罕布什尔州乡村一个名叫Grafton的小镇，那里的人口已经少于1,000。他们刚刚出现并开始接手镇政府，并摆脱了所有可能的规章制度和税收开支。
    他们不是在真空中选择它。他们实际上进行了非常仔细和彻底的搜索。他们很快进入了新罕布什尔州，因为那是“现场免费或死亡”状态。他们知道这将与他们的个人主义和个人责任理念保持一致。但是，一旦他们决定选择新罕布什尔州，他们实际上就访问了数十个小镇，寻找能够使他们接手的完美因素组合。
 他们需要的是一个足够小的小镇，可以抬起头来弯腰现有的公民，在土地便宜的地方，他们可以进来并购买一堆土地，并接待新来的殖民者。他们想要一个没有分区的地方，因为他们希望能够生活在非传统的住房环境中，而不必经历建造或购买昂贵房屋或既有房屋的繁琐工作。
    格拉夫顿（Grafton）人民很快发现，非传统的住房状况意味着在树林中扎营或一堆运输集装箱或其他物品。他们带来了蒙古包和活动房屋，并形成了几排小木屋和帐篷。在一个叫“帐篷城”的地方，一群人每天都住在帐篷里。他们都遵循广泛的“人身自由”原则团结起来，但是正如您所期望的那样，他们在行使自由方面有很多差异。
  小组的人口统计情况如何？我们主要是在谈论在互联网上找到对方的白人或艾恩·兰德兄弟吗？
  好吧，我们谈论的是数百人，尽管数字还不是很清楚。他们肯定歪歪了男性。他们肯定偏白。他们中的一些人有很多钱，这使他们能够自由扎根并搬到新罕布什尔州的一个小镇。他们中的许多人几乎没有钱，也没有什么可以留在原地。这样他们就可以接机。但是其中大多数人没有家庭状况或9到5份工作，而这实际上是他们比其他任何地方都更有特色的地方。 
刚露面时，他们并没有告诉任何人他们正在这样做，只是社区中有几个富有同情心的自由主义者。因此，格拉夫顿的人们突然意识到，他们的城镇正受到一群理想主义自由主义者的侵袭。他们很生气。他们开了一个大城镇会议。这是一个非常shouty，非常生气镇民大会，在此期间，他们告诉自由外地人谁也不敢来了，他们不希望他们在那里，他们并不欣赏被视为如果他们的社区是一个实验围栏的自由主义者来并尝试证明一些东西。
 但是，尽管自由主义者从未超过现有的格拉夫顿居民，但他们发现他们可以进驻，而且他们可以找到志趣相投的人，传统保守派或只是非常以自由为导向的人，他们就足够的问题与他们达成了一致。尽管遭到了愤怒的反对，但他们仍然能够在政府的杠杆下发挥自己的意志。
 他们无法通过他们想要的一些计划。他们试图从学区撤出并完全停止支付道路维修费用，或者宣布格拉夫顿为联合国自由区，但没有成功，其中有些是类似的事情。但他们确实发现，许多现有Grafton居民很乐意将城镇服务削减到最低限度。因此，他们成功地控制了警察，道路服务和消防服务，甚至公共图书馆。所有这些东西都被砍掉了。
     几乎可以用任何衡量标准来衡量一个小镇的成功程度，格拉夫顿情况变得更糟。回收率下降了。邻居抱怨上升了。该镇的法律费用上升，因为他们不断为自己免受Free Towners的诉讼辩护。居住在该镇的性罪犯人数有所增加。记录在案的犯罪数量有所增加。该镇从未以谋杀案谋杀过，并且由于室友之争而发生了前两次谋杀案。
 因此，各种各样的负面后果开始出现。同时，通常希望通过强大的警察部队来解决这些问题的小镇反而发现它被困了。因此，该镇只有一名专职警察和一名警察局长，他必须站起来在镇上开会，并告诉人们他不能将巡洋舰放置在马路上数周，因为他没有有钱修理它并使它成为安全的车辆。
     事实证明，如果有一群人在非传统的生活环境中生活在树林中，每个人都以自己的方式管理食物，以自己的方式管理他们的废物流，那么您实质上就是在教该地区的熊每个人的居住环境都像一个难题，必须解开才能释放热量。因此，该地区的熊开始注意到房屋中存在卡路里的事实。
 自由镇居民鼓励熊的一件事是无意的，因为他们只是把浪费扔掉了。他们不希望政府告诉他们如何管理潜在的熊市引诱剂。另一种方式是故意的，因为有些人只是为了看熊吃东西而感到高兴和高兴地开始喂它们。 
可以想象，事情变得凌乱，小镇无法应对。有些人在射击熊。有些人在喂熊。一些人在他们的财产上设置了诱杀装置，以试图通过痛苦阻止熊。其他人则向他们扔鞭炮。其他人则将辣椒粉放在他们的垃圾上，这样当熊嗅它们的垃圾时，他们会得到充满胡椒粉的口鼻部。
   我们正在专门谈论黑熊。对于那里的非熊专家来说，黑熊对人类没有侵略性。但是格拉夫顿的熊却……不同。
  熊是非常聪明的解决问题的动物。他们可以真正思考解决问题的方式。这就是让他们在格拉夫顿进取的原因。在这种情况下，一只合理的熊会明白有很多食物，因为胆大一点会得到回报。因此，他们开始积极突击搜查食物，当一个人出现时，逃跑的可能性就降低了。
 熊书中有许多很好的例子，它们以大胆，异常激进的方式行事，但在2012年达到顶峰，当时格拉夫顿镇发生黑熊袭击。这似乎并不罕见，但实际上，新罕布什尔州至少有100年没有遭受黑熊袭击。因此整个州从未见过一次熊袭击事件，现在在格拉夫顿，一名妇女在家中遭到黑熊袭击。
 然后，几年后，第二名妇女遭到袭击，不是在格拉夫顿，而是在附近的一个城镇。由于这本书是撰写和出版的，因此实际上是在新罕布什尔州的同一地区和同一地区，发生了第三次熊袭击。而且我认为很明显，除非有所改变，否则还会有更多的熊袭击。
  “很容易陷入这样的陷阱，即相信只要每个人都遵循这一原则，社会就会成为这个完美的系统。”
  您对本书中提到的自由主义者持公平，甚至同情的态度，但我确实想知道您是否逐渐将他们视为狂热分子。 
您知道，“自由主义者”对于各种各样的人来说是一个很奇怪的总称。一些自由主义者是围绕白人至上和种族主义的思想而建立的。这些自由主义者并非如此。我遇到的大多数自由主义者都是善良，体面的人，在任何给定的时刻都会与邻居大方。但总的来说，当他们在城镇会议上时，他们将投票反对切断邻居的耕作，以伤害那个邻居。
 因此，我想我注意到他们的性格或日常互动与他们的哲学和政治运动的更广泛含义之间存在奇怪的脱节。不确定我会使用“狂热”一词，但肯定是一个奇怪的断连。
  对于任何有兴趣看到它的人来说，都有一个教训，那就是，如果您试图将世界整洁地放入意识形态盒子中，那么您必须歪曲或忽视现实才能做到这一点-通常会带来可怕的后果。
  是的，我认为这对自由主义和所有生活哲学都是如此。很容易陷入这样一个陷阱，即相信只有每个人都遵循这一原则，社会才能成为这个完美的系统。
  由于格拉夫顿（Grafton）发生的事情，这个故事中的任何人物都没有怀疑他们的自由主义吗？还是主要是认为自由主义不会失败，只能失败？
  这本书的中心人物之一是名叫约翰·巴比阿兹（John Babiarz）的消防员。约翰有在自由主义者平台上竞选新罕布什尔州州长的名声，并且比美国其他州长的自由主义者候选人做得更好。他邀请自由主义者进来并开始自由城项目。他是他们的本地人脉。
 但是，在项目结束时（2016年某个时候），他确实在自己和来到城镇的许多极端自由主义者之间做出了一些区分。他仍然认为自己是一个自由主义者，并且是一个非常虔诚的人，但是到项目结束时，他与大多数其他自由主义者都不合。它表明，除非您真正拥有一个由自由主义者经营的社区，否则很难说它是什么或它将是什么样。 
最后，您是否认为这些人违反了自由主义的极限，还是更多地是关于特定地区特定人群的愚蠢？  我认为他们与自由主义的愚蠢相撞。 我确实确实认为，存在着一堵坚硬的现实壁垒，这将挫败任何大规模实施自由主义的努力。 而且我认为，如果您给自由主义者以魔杖，并让他们以他们想要的方式改变社会，那将不会像他们想象的那样起作用，而且我认为它会像格拉夫顿一样崩溃。  理解具有巨大的力量。 Vox回答您最重要的问题，并为您提供清晰的信息，以帮助您理解日益混乱的世界。 对Vox的财务捐助将帮助我们继续为依赖我们的数百万人提供免费的说明性新闻。 请考虑立即向Vox捐款，最低$ 3。
note_en:
Every ideology produces its own brand of fanatics, but there’s something special about libertarianism.
 I don’t mean that as an insult, either. I love libertarians! For the most part, they’re fun and interesting people. But they also tend to be cocksure about core principles in a way most people aren’t. If you’ve ever encountered a freshly minted Ayn Rand enthusiast, you know what I mean.
 And yet one of the things that makes political philosophy so amusing is that it’s mostly abstract. You can’t really prove anything — it’s just a never-ending argument about values. Every now and again, though, reality intervenes in a way that illustrates the absurdity of particular ideas.
 Something like this happened in the mid-2000s in a small New Hampshire town called Grafton. Matthew Hongoltz-Hetling, author of a new book titled   A Libertarian Walks Into a Bear, says it’s the “boldest social experiment in modern American history.” I don’t know if it’s the “boldest,” but it’s definitely one of the strangest.
 The experiment was called the “Free Town Project” (it later became the “Free State Project”), and the goal was simple: take over Grafton’s local government and turn it into a libertarian utopia. The movement was cooked up by a small group of ragtag libertarian activists who saw in Grafton a unique opportunity to realize their dreams of a perfectly logical and perfectly market-based community. Needless to say, utopia never arrived, but the bears did! (I promise I’ll explain below.)
 I reached out to Hongoltz-Hetling to talk about his book. I wanted to know what happened in New Hampshire, why the experiment failed, and what the whole saga can teach us not just about libertarianism but about the dangers of loving theory more than reality.
   How would you describe the “Free Town Project” to someone who doesn’t know anything about it?
  I’d put it like this: There’s a national community of libertarians that has developed over the last 40 or 50 years, and they’ve never really had a place to call their own. They’ve never been in charge of a nation, or a state, or even a city. And they’ve always really wanted to create a community that would showcase what would happen if they implemented their principles on a broad scale.
 So in 2004, a group of them decided that they wanted to take some action on this deficiency, and they decided to launch what they called the Free Town Project. They sent out a call to a bunch of loosely affiliated national libertarians and told everyone to move to this one spot and found this utopian community that would then serve as a shining jewel for the world to see that libertarian philosophies worked not only in theory but in practice. And they chose a town in rural New Hampshire called Grafton that already had fewer than 1,000 people in it. And they just showed up and started working to take over the town government and get rid of every rule and regulation and tax expense that they could.
    They didn’t choose it in a vacuum. They actually conducted a very careful and thorough search. They zeroed in on the state of New Hampshire fairly quickly because that’s the “Live Free or Die” state. They knew that it would align well with their philosophy of individualism and personal responsibility. But once they decided on New Hampshire, they actually visited dozens of small towns, looking for that perfect mix of factors that would enable them to take over.
 What they needed was a town that was small enough that they could come up and elbow the existing citizenry, someplace where land was cheap, where they could come in and buy up a bunch of land and kind of host their incoming colonists. And they wanted a place that had no zoning, because they wanted to be able to live in nontraditional housing situations and not have to go through the rigamarole of building or buying expensive homes or preexisting homes.
    As the people of Grafton soon found out, a nontraditional housing situation meant a camp in the woods or a bunch of shipping containers or whatever. They brought in yurts and mobile homes and formed little clusters of cabins and tents. There was one location called “Tent City,” where a bunch of people just lived in tents from day to day. They all united under this broad umbrella principle of “personal freedom,” but as you’d expect, there was a lot of variation in how they exercised it.
  What did the demographics of the group look like? Are we talking mostly about white guys or Ayn Rand bros who found each other on the internet?
  Well, we’re talking about hundreds of people, though the numbers aren’t all that clear. They definitely skewed male. They definitely skewed white. Some of them had a lot of money, which gave them the freedom to be able to pick up roots and move to a small town in New Hampshire. A lot of them had very little money and nothing keeping them in their places. So they were able to pick up and come in. But most of them just didn’t have those family situations or those 9-to-5 jobs, and that was really what characterized them more than anything else.
    When they first showed up, they hadn’t told anyone that they were doing this, with the exception of a couple of sympathetic libertarians within the community. And so all of a sudden the people in Grafton woke up to the fact that their town was in the process of being invaded by a bunch of idealistic libertarians. And they were pissed. They had a big town meeting. It was a very shouty, very angry town meeting, during which they told the Free Towners who dared to come that they didn’t want them there and they didn’t appreciate being treated as if their community was an experimental playpen for libertarians to come in and try to prove something.
 But the libertarians, even though they never outnumbered the existing Grafton residents, what they found was that they could come in, and they could find like-minded people, traditional conservatives or just very liberty-oriented individuals, who agreed with them on enough issues that, despite that angry opposition, they were able to start to work their will on the levers of government.
 They couldn’t pass some of the initiatives they wanted. They tried unsuccessfully to withdraw from the school district and to completely discontinue paying for road repairs, or to declare Grafton a United Nations free zone, some of the outlandish things like that. But they did find that a lot of existing Grafton residents would be happy to cut town services to the bone. And so they successfully put a stranglehold on things like police services, things like road services and fire services and even the public library. All of these things were cut to the bone.
     By pretty much any measure you can look at to gauge a town’s success, Grafton got worse. Recycling rates went down. Neighbor complaints went up. The town’s legal costs went up because they were constantly defending themselves from lawsuits from Free Towners. The number of sex offenders living in the town went up. The number of recorded crimes went up. The town had never had a murder in living memory, and it had its first two, a double homicide, over a roommate dispute.
 So there were all sorts of negative consequences that started to crop up. And meanwhile, the town that would ordinarily want to address these things, say with a robust police force, instead found that it was hamstrung. So the town only had one full-time police officer, a single police chief, and he had to stand up at town meeting and tell people that he couldn’t put his cruiser on the road for a period of weeks because he didn’t have money to repair it and make it a safe vehicle.
     It turns out that if you have a bunch of people living in the woods in nontraditional living situations, each of which is managing food in their own way and their waste streams in their own way, then you’re essentially teaching the bears in the region that every human habitation is like a puzzle that has to be solved in order to unlock its caloric payload. And so the bears in the area started to take notice of the fact that there were calories available in houses.
 One thing that the Free Towners did that encouraged the bears was unintentional, in that they just threw their waste out how they wanted. They didn’t want the government to tell them how to manage their potential bear attractants. The other way was intentional, in that some people just started feeding the bears just for the joy and pleasure of watching them eat.
 As you can imagine, things got messy and there was no way for the town to deal with it. Some people were shooting the bears. Some people were feeding the bears. Some people were setting booby traps on their properties in an effort to deter the bears through pain. Others were throwing firecrackers at them. Others were putting cayenne pepper on their garbage so that when the bears sniffed their garbage, they would get a snout full of pepper.
   We’re talking about black bears specifically. For the non-bear experts out there, black bears are not known to be aggressive toward humans. But the bears in Grafton were ... different.
  Bears are very smart problem-solving animals. They can really think their way through problems. And that was what made them aggressive in Grafton. In this case, a reasonable bear would understand that there was food to be had, that it was going to be rewarded for being bolder. So they started aggressively raiding food and became less likely to run away when a human showed up.
 There are lots of great examples in the book of bears acting in bold, unusually aggressive manners, but it culminated in 2012, when there was a black bear attack in the town of Grafton. That might not seem that unusual, but, in fact, New Hampshire had not had a black bear attack for at least 100 years leading up to that. So the whole state had never seen a single bear attack, and now here in Grafton, a woman was attacked in her home by a black bear.
 And then, a few years after that, a second woman was attacked, not in Grafton but in a neighboring town. And since the book was written and published, there’s actually been a third bear attack, also in the same little cluster and the same little region of New Hampshire. And I think it’s very clear that, unless something changes, more bear attacks will come.
  “It’s very easy to fall into this trap of believing that if only everybody followed this or that principle, then society would become this perfect system”
  You’re fair, even sympathetic, to the libertarians you profile in this book, but I do wonder if you came to see them increasingly as fanatics.
  You know, “libertarian” is such a weird umbrella term for a very diverse group of people. Some libertarians are built around the idea of white supremacy and racism. That was not the case with these libertarians. Most of the libertarians that I met were kind, decent people who would be generous with a neighbor in any given moment. But in the abstract, when they’re at a town meeting, they will vote to hurt that neighbor by cutting off, say, support for road plowing.
 So I guess what I noticed is a strange disconnect between their personalities or their day-to-day interactions and the broader implications of their philosophies and their political movement. Not sure I’d use the word “fanatic,” but definitely a weird disconnect.
  There’s a lesson in this for anyone interested in seeing it, which is that if you try to make the world fit neatly into an ideological box, you’ll have to distort or ignore reality to do it — usually with terrible consequences.
  Yeah, I think that’s true for libertarianism and really all philosophies of life. It’s very easy to fall into this trap of believing that if only everybody followed this or that principle, then society would become this perfect system.
  Did any of the characters in this story come to doubt their libertarianism as a result of what happened in Grafton? Or was it mostly a belief that libertarianism can’t fail, it can only be failed?
  One of the central characters in the book is a firefighter named John Babiarz. And John had the distinction of running for the governor of New Hampshire on the libertarian platform, and did better than any other gubernatorial libertarian candidate has ever done in America. And he invited the libertarians to come in and begin the Free Town Project. He was their local connection.
 But by the end of the project [sometime in 2016], he had really drawn some distinctions between himself and many of the extremist libertarians who came to town. He still considers himself to be a libertarian, and a very devout one at that, but by the end of the project he was at odds with most of the other libertarians. And it shows that until you actually have a libertarian-run community, it’s very hard to say what it is or what it will look like.
  In the end, do you think these people bumped up against the limits of libertarianism, or is this more about the particular follies of a particular group of people in a particular place?
  I think they bumped up against the follies of libertarianism. I really do think that there is a hard wall of reality that exists that’s going to foil any effort to implement libertarianism on a broad scale. And I think if you gave a libertarian the magic wand and allowed them to transform society the way that they wanted to, it wouldn’t work the way they imagined, and I think it would break down just as Grafton did.

 吉打州地方官员哈基姆·阿里夫·穆德·诺尔（Hakim Ariff Md Noor）表示，Ffira Mikah体育场的新名字倒是他的名字，这只是一个巧合。
   “与我的名字无关。这个小型体育场的名称具有隐含含义。这个名字是在一次行政会议上选定并提出的。”他告诉马来西亚报纸Sinar Harian。
 “用西班牙语或希腊语，＆＃39; Ffira＆＃39;意味着伟大的事物，而＆mikah＆＃39;在阿拉伯语中加上天使的名字。”
   尽管“ mikah”类似于阿拉伯语中的“ malaikah”，但“ ffira”在西班牙语或希腊语中并不意味着“伟大”，也似乎没有任何其他含义。
  由Padang Terap区议会拥有的Ffira Mikah体育场在获得价值37,000英镑的升级费用后，于上周由Ariff先生揭幕。

A  Malaysian official who renamed a stadium insists its new title, which is his name in reverse, is in fact an amalgam of  Arabic and “Spanish or Greek”.
 Hakim Ariff Md Noor, the district officer for the state of Kedah, said it was just a coincidence that the Ffira Mikah stadium’s new name was his name backwards.
   “It has nothing to do with my name. The name of this mini stadium has an implicit meaning. The name was chosen and proposed in an administrative meeting,” he told Malaysian newspaper   Sinar Harian.
 “In Spanish or Greek, 'Ffira' means something great, while 'Mikah' in Arabic in conjunction with the name of an angel.”
   While “mikah” resembles the Arabic word for angel, “malaikah”, “ffira” does not mean “great” in Spanish or Greek, nor does it appear to mean any other word.
  The Ffira Mikah stadium, which is owned by Padang Terap District Council, was inaugurated by Mr Ariff last week after it received £37,000-worth of upgrades.
  “I want to make the stadium a centre of excellence to revitalise football as a district product,” Judge Ariff said. “The filling of the stadium is more important than its name.”

 He said the stadium would be renamed again in order to avoid allegations of impropriety.
  “After this, it will be known as the Kuala Nerang Mini Stadium,” he said.
 The “Ffira Mikah” sign over the pitch has reportedly now been removed by KN Kiara Football Club, which manages the stadium.
